title: dimocarpus fumatus subsp. fumatus
intro: Dimocarpus fumatus subsp. fumatus, also known as the velvet seed or velvet bean, is a plant species native to tropical regions of Asia. The plant contains medicinal properties, primarily due to its high concentration of L-tryptophan, an amino acid that serves as a precursor to serotonin. The velvet bean offers various health benefits, including reduced anxiety and improved mood, due to its serotonin-boosting properties, as well as potential antidiabetic and antihelmintic effects. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including dried seeds, extracts, and teas, although its use should be strictly regulated and under medical supervision due to potential toxicity.
